Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Add 6 psychedelic species: mexicana, tampanensis, caerulescens, stuntzii, aeruginascens, salicinus (#3)

New species with full content, images, and maps:
- Psilocybe mexicana (Teonanácatl) — the original sacred mushroom
- Psilocybe tampanensis (Philosopher's Stone) — rarest psilocybin species
- Psilocybe caerulescens (Derrumbe) — Mazatec landslide mushroom
- Psilocybe stuntzii (Blue Ringer) — Pacific Northwest urban species
- Inocybe aeruginascens (Green-Staining Inocybe) — psilocybin outlier
- Pluteus salicinus (Willow Shield) — wood-rotting psilocybin producer

All with CC-licensed Wikimedia Commons images and GBIF distribution maps.

Co-authored-by: Varun Vaid <varunvaid@Varuns-MacBook-Air.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Proof of concept: strategy-conditioned PPO on LunarLander

Implements and validates the core idea — a single actor-critic network
learns distinct behaviours (speed, fuel efficiency, precision) by
conditioning on a strategy input vector and weighting per-strategy
reward signals with the same vector.

Trained for 1.5M timesteps with PPO. Results show clear behavioural
differentiation: speed strategy uses ~24% more fuel, fuel-efficiency
strategy achieves highest landing rate (82%) with least fuel (51.2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: adaptive-strategy-rl
repository.description: Strategy-conditioned RL: teaching one neural network multiple play-styles via strategy-vector conditioning. POC on LunarLander, targeting Rocket League.

commit.message:
fix: use deterministic fallback scenario ID to prevent duplicates

When cloud is empty on sign-in, handleSignIn creates a fallback scenario
with a random UUID. If racing code paths (auth events, cross-tab sync)
both trigger this, two different UUIDs get saved as separate rows.

Fix: use the user's own UUID as the fallback scenario ID. Since both
paths derive the same ID, the upsert (onConflict: 'id') deduplicates
into a single row regardless of race timing.

feat: add stringer baseline CLI commands (#265)

feat: add stringer baseline commands [stringer-kv9.2] [stringer-kv9.3] [stringer-kv9.4] [stringer-kv9.6]

Add `stringer baseline` parent command with five subcommands:

- `create [path]`: runs a full scan and writes all signal IDs to
  .stringer/baseline.json with configurable reason, --collectors
  scoping, and --force for overwrite
- `suppress <signal-id>`: adds/updates a single suppression with
  --reason, --comment, and --expires flags; validates signal ID format
  (str-[0-9a-f]{8})
- `list`: table output with ID, Reason, Comment, SuppressedBy, Age
  columns; supports --json, --reason filter, and --expired filter
- `remove <signal-id>`: removes one suppression by ID; --expired
  removes all expired suppressions at once
- `status`: shows total suppressions, breakdown by reason, expired
  count, oldest/newest dates; warns when >20% are expired

All subcommands use baseline.Load()/Save() from internal/baseline.
Includes comprehensive tests covering valid/invalid IDs, filters,
edge cases (empty baseline, all filtered out), and helper functions.

Signed-off-by: Dave Tashner <5702882+davetashner@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix FUP reset offset: use local session totals instead of central's

The central's resetFup() calculates fup_data_offset from its own sessions,
but the node has different session totals (more data). This caused the
offset to be too low on the node, making fup_data_used stay high even
after reset.

Now when the node detects a FUP reset via pull sync, it recalculates
fup_data_offset from its own local sessions, ensuring fup_data_used
starts at 0 correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: manual code exchange with full-page navigation to bypass state bug

The library's built-in code handler silently swallows errors (void IIFE,
no catch), leaving isLoading stuck at true. The previous manual approach
hung because it relied on React state (isAuthenticated) to trigger
redirect, but setToken updates don't propagate from the signIn call.

Fix: exchange the code manually, then use window.location.href (full
page reload) instead of router.replace. This sidesteps the React state
propagation issue entirely — tokens are in localStorage, and the fresh
page load picks them up cleanly.

chore: switch npm publish to OIDC trusted publisher

- Remove NPM_TOKEN dependency, use npm trusted publisher (OIDC) instead
- Add permissions: id-token: write for OIDC token exchange

commit.message:
Fix stale follow request notifications after request cancelled

When a user cancels a follow request (unfollows) or blocks someone,
the follow_request notification was left behind with active Accept/Decline
buttons. Clicking Accept returned 404 silently because the relationship
no longer existed.

- Delete follow_request notifications when unfollowing (request cancelled)
- Delete follow_request notifications both directions when blocking
- Frontend: show "This request is no longer active" on 404 instead of
  silently resetting the Accept button

commit.message:
docs: explain how secrets interact with the KServe inference service

Add a dedicated 'Secrets & the Inference Service' section to README.md
covering both secret patterns used across the KServe notebooks:

- Pattern 1 (storage secret): notebook reads Azure storage key via
  Managed Identity, writes it to a K8s Opaque secret, KServe's storage
  initializer sidecar reads it to download the model from Azure Blob.
  Includes the mlpipeline-minio-artifact patch/restore flow.

- Pattern 2 (ACR pull secret): AAD token → ACR refresh token →
  repo-scoped access token → dockerconfigjson K8s secret →
  imagePullSecrets on the ISVC → kubelet pulls image from private ACR.

Each pattern has an ASCII flow diagram, a pod lifecycle diagram shows
where both secrets are consumed, and a side-by-side comparison table
summarises the key differences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix confirmed-removal misclassification and HTML resource leak

1. Confirmed document deletions (404) were being classified as IGNORE
   because the summary contained "removed from" which matched the
   rotation-suppression rule. Now checks for "confirmed not found" to
   let real deletions through.

2. HTML pages linked from What's New were tracked as document resources,
   causing false NEW DOCUMENT alerts for existing pages like REET.
   Rotating feeds now only track file downloads (PDFs, docs, etc.) as
   resources — HTML pages are handled by child page discovery.

3. Added two regression tests: confirmed-not-found removal classification
   and What's New PDF-only resource tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: fan-out/fan-in/map-reduce pipeline capabilities (#274)

* docs: fan-out/fan-in/map-reduce design document

* docs: fan-out/fan-in implementation plan

* chore: add .worktrees to gitignore for worktree isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add concurrent execution to step.foreach via concurrency config

Adds optional worker-pool concurrency to ForEachStep via two new config keys:
- concurrency: int (0 = sequential, default; N = N-worker pool)
- error_strategy: "fail_fast" (default) or "collect_errors"

When concurrency > 0, executeConcurrent() spawns goroutines bounded by a
channel semaphore. Results are pre-allocated by index to preserve order.
Each goroutine deep-copies the PipelineContext via buildChildContext() for
isolation. Backward compatible: concurrency=0 or omitted uses existing
sequential path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add step.parallel for concurrent fixed-branch fan-out

* feat: register step.parallel in plugin, add schemas and inference

* feat: add collection template functions (sum, pluck, flatten, unique, groupBy, sortBy, first, last, min, max)

Adds 10 collection functions to the pipeline template engine for inline
aggregation of slice data. All functions accept []any (or any slice type
via reflect) and are composable via Go template pipelines.

Helper functions added before templateFuncMap():
- toAnySlice: reflect-based conversion for any slice type
- extractField: pulls a named key from map elements (for key-based variants)

Functions with optional KEY parameter (sum, min, max, unique) operate on
scalar slices or map-field extraction. sortBy and groupBy operate on slices
of maps. first/last return single elements (nil renders as <no value>).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add collection functions to LSP template completions

Registers the 10 new collection template functions (sum, pluck, flatten,
unique, groupBy, sortBy, first, last, min, max) in lsp/registry.go so
they appear in template expression completions.

DOCUMENTATION.md and step table entries were already updated by Task 4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add step.parallel, concurrent foreach, and collection functions to docs and LSP

* docs: add parallel fan-out example config

Demonstrates all three new capabilities in a single runnable example:
1. step.parallel with collect_errors — parallel API aggregation fan-out
2. step.foreach with concurrency: 5 and error_strategy: collect_errors — concurrent batch processing
3. Collection template functions (sum, max, min, unique, groupBy, pluck) — map/reduce stats endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite the error handling loop in pipeline_step_foreach.go to use a switch
statement instead of if-else-if-else chain, as recommended by gocritic linter.
